title: Explain synchronized blocks vs synchronized methods in Java.

answers.junior: Synchronized methods lock the whole method. Synchronized blocks lock only part of the code.
answers.middle: Synchronized blocks allow finer control, locking only critical sections. Methods lock the entire method, which can reduce performance.
answers.senior: Use synchronized blocks for granular locking and better performance. Methods are easier but less flexible. Always lock the smallest scope needed to avoid contention.
answers.tricky: Prefer synchronized blocks for complex logic. Analyze lock scope and contention. Use java.util.concurrent locks for advanced scenarios. Avoid locking on 'this' in public methods to prevent external interference.
